name: uiux-ops
description: UI/UX 設計・改善を進めるための運用手順とチェックリストをまとめたスキル。
capabilities: Provide UI/UX design principles and best practices
Create consistent design patterns and component guidelines
Check designs for WCAG accessibility compliance
Define design systems with color, typography, and spacing rules
Guide interaction design with feedback and error handling patterns
Maintain documentation for design decisions and specifications
author: A-Genno
securitySummary: All 14 static findings evaluated as false positives. Scanner flagged legitimate JSON metadata fields (URLs, hashes, timestamps), markdown code formatting (backticks for file references), and typical text entropy patterns. This skill contains only documentation and operational guidelines for UI/UX design with no executable code, network calls, or external command execution.
valueStatement: UI/UX design projects often lack consistent standards and clear processes, leading to fragmented user experiences and accessibility gaps. This skill provides comprehensive operational procedures and checklists to streamline design workflows, ensure consistency, and maintain accessibility compliance across all projects.
limitations: Does not generate design assets or mockups
Does not conduct user research or usability testing
Does not replace professional designers for complex design decisions

useCases.title: Establishing design standards for a new product
useCases.description: A product manager needs to define consistent UI patterns and design principles before development begins. The skill provides guidelines for creating a design system with reusable components, color palettes, and typography rules.

useCases.title: Auditing existing interfaces for accessibility
useCases.description: A developer wants to ensure their application meets accessibility requirements. The skill provides WCAG-based checklists and guidelines to identify and fix accessibility issues in current interfaces.

useCases.title: Improving user interaction patterns
useCases.description: A designer wants to enhance user satisfaction by adding better feedback mechanisms and error handling. The skill provides interaction design principles for creating intuitive user experiences.

promptTemplates.title: Getting started with design principles
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the core principles of user-centered design for web applications? Include guidance on consistency, accessibility, and responsive design.

promptTemplates.title: Creating a design system
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a design system for a SaaS application. Include guidance on component library structure, color palette definition, typography rules, and spacing conventions.

promptTemplates.title: Accessibility audit checklist
promptTemplates.prompt: Provide a comprehensive checklist for auditing web interfaces against WCAG 2.1 AA guidelines. Include specific items for color contrast, keyboard navigation, screen reader support, and focus management.

promptTemplates.title: Interaction design patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain best practices for handling user interactions including visual feedback for actions, error message design, loading state communication, and micro-interactions. Provide examples of each pattern.

bestPractices: Follow user-centered design principles by focusing on user goals and tasks first
Maintain consistency across all screens using a documented design system
Validate accessibility early and often using WCAG guidelines as a baseline
antiPatterns: Using different interaction patterns for similar actions across screens
Hiding important information behind hover states or accordions
Relying solely on color to convey information without additional cues

faq.question: Does this skill generate actual design files or code?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidelines, checklists, and best practices for UI/UX design. It does not generate design assets, mockups, or implementation code.

faq.question: What accessibility standards does this skill cover?
faq.answer: The skill references WCAG 2.1 guidelines, focusing on perceivable, operable, understandable, and robust principles for web accessibility.

faq.question: Can this skill help me create a complete design system?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides guidance on defining component libraries, color palettes, typography, spacing systems, and design tokens for building design systems.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for mobile app design?
faq.answer: Yes. The principles of user-centered design, consistency, and accessibility apply to mobile applications. The skill includes responsive design guidance that adapts to different screen sizes.

faq.question: How does this skill handle error messages?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidelines for writing clear, actionable error messages that explain what went wrong and how to fix it, with accessibility considerations for screen readers.

faq.question: Does this skill include information about user testing?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on design principles and operational guidelines. It does not cover user research methodologies or usability testing procedures.
